findings.title: Reviewer lane can mint and spend parent writeback authority by taking first truth snapshot
findings.rationale: `capture_review_truth_snapshot` mints and returns the plaintext `writeback_authority_token` to any caller without checking caller role/lane ownership. `record-review-outcome` only requires that a snapshot is present and internally consistent, and reviewer identity checks can be bypassed by choosing a non-child reviewer string; the self-writeback heuristic only flags reviewer-output refs that encode lane-like prefixes, while canonical refs from `record-reviewer-output` are `reviewer-output:<bundle-id>:<output-id>`. This allows a reviewer lane that captures the first snapshot for a bundle to fabricate parent-owned writeback authority and clear the gate. Existing tests cover remint rejection after first capture but do not cover unauthorized first-capture/use.
findings.suggested_next_action: Bind `capture-review-truth-snapshot` and `record-review-outcome` to parent review-loop authority (for example an active parent lease/owner nonce) and reject snapshots not minted by that parent context; also add a regression test proving a reviewer lane cannot capture the initial truth snapshot nor successfully call `record-review-outcome` with self-produced reviewer-output evidence.
